what_it_does: Shared runtime for every DNA surface: hex/RGB/HSL color math, warmth and polarity surface generation, CSS custom-property injection, persistence API helpers, an estate-wide Atlas overlay, story-tree traversal, control rendering from component records, typed feedback marks, spacing/role resolution from registries, and reusable split-pane and pinch-zoom gesture mechanisms. Exports everything under window.DNA via Object.assign so earlier or later scripts can share the namespace.
observations: Implements hex-to-RGB, RGB-to-hex, hex-to-HSL, HSL-to-hex, and linear hex interpolation.
Provides invertL, a lightness-only inversion used to derive dark-polarity surfaces while preserving luminance deltas.
surfaces() interpolates between cool and warm anchors on a warmth dial, then optionally inverts for dark polarity.
injectVars writes a large set of CSS variables onto a target root, applying invertL only to luminance-role colors and holding chroma accents.
Contains a hardcoded fallback color object FB between DNA:FALLBACKS START/END markers.
Provides async fetch wrappers _get/_post and an api object covering feedback, tokens, grammar, layouts, sequence, and generic dna endpoints.
mountAtlas injects inline styles and DOM elements for a fixed-position navigation panel driven by surface/atlas.json.
story.node and story.cut traverse a story tree by id or by named tier cut.
control() renders a button from application.components records using state-defined geometry and color values.
mark/marksOf convert legacy feedback entries into typed {target, kind, value} records.
dialsFor and spaceDialMult read grammar.dials.*.targets.modifies to compute spacing multipliers.
space() and injectSpace() resolve spacing from grammar scales, bonds, density dials, and tier.
resolve() walks dotted pointers against supplied registries to resolve role colour and type, but flags space as unresolved pending per-relationship tuning.
splitRatio derives work/panel grow ratios from grammar proportion archetypes and application viewer_layout configuration.
splitPane and pinchZoom are generic, reusable UI mechanisms with pointer/touch event handling and optional localStorage persistence for the split fraction.
standouts: The invertL/surfaces/injectVars trio forms the core 'derive dark from light' logic.
Generic splitPane and pinchZoom are designed as reusable mechanisms rather than single-surface code.
resolve() is a registry-pointer walker that explicitly defers space resolution.
The Atlas overlay is mounted on every surface from one registry.
conventions: Namespaced under DNA.* and merged with Object.assign to avoid clobbering prior exports.
Pure functions take loaded registries as arguments rather than fetching internally.
CSS variables are set via root.style.setProperty.
Comments are dense and reference design packets, sections, and prior decisions.
Uses var(..., fallback) so the Atlas chip works even when surface.css is absent.
concerns: The hardcoded FB fallback object may drift if not regenerated from dna/tokens.json.
mountAtlas accesses document.body and document.head directly, so it will fail if the DOM is not ready.
_get/_post throw on network errors but do not offer retry or offline handling.
resolve() leaves space unresolved; consumers must inspect the unresolved array.
The regex in walk() rejects pointers containing uppercase letters in the first segment, though the case-insensitive flag allows the rest.
notes: This is the shared substrate: load it once, then build surfaces on its color, spacing, story, and gesture primitives.
questions: What is the exact schema of tokens.warmth_anchors and tokens.color expected by injectVars?
How is the FB fallback regenerated and kept in sync with dna/tokens.json?
Which surfaces call mountAtlas and what currentId values do they pass?
What is the intended consumer of DNA.story.cut beyond the listed tier names?
How are grammar.dials multipliers and scale_tiers structured in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a shared runtime providing color, surface, API, story, control, spacing, and gesture utilities for all DNA surfaces, as the header states.
apparent_themes: Derive-never-place: compute values from declared registries.
Shared runtime / single source of truth.
Polarity as luminance inversion preserving magnitude.
Mechanism over use: reusable UI primitives.
intention_signals: To create one portable, load-order-safe JavaScript substrate that every surface can rely on, so color, spacing, story structure, and common UI mechanics are computed from declared DNA registries rather than reimplemented per page.
novelty: The combination of a warmth-dial lerp, a magnitude-preserving luminance inversion for dark mode, and a registry-pointer role resolver in one shared runtime is unusual; the generic splitPane/pinchZoom 'mechanism not use' approach is also notable.
contribution: A centralized, reusable set of color, spacing, story, control, feedback, and gesture utilities that surfaces can compose without reimplementing core logic.
summary_for_embedding: Shared JavaScript runtime for DNA surfaces providing color math, warmth/polarity surface generation, CSS variable injection, API persistence, an Atlas overlay, story traversal, control rendering, feedback marks, spacing/role resolution, and reusable split-pane/pinch-zoom gestures.
intention_for_embedding: To give every DNA surface a common, registry-driven substrate for color, spacing, content structure, and UI mechanics.

symbols.does: Parses a hex color string into an [r,g,b] integer array.
symbols.description: Normalizes the input, expands 3-digit shorthand, and returns red, green, and blue components as integers in the 0-255 range.

symbols.does: Converts integer RGB components to an uppercase #RRGGBB string.
symbols.description: Clamps each component to [0,255], rounds it, and formats it as two-digit hexadecimal, prefixed with #.

symbols.does: Local helper that formats one RGB channel as two hex digits.
symbols.description: Used inside rgbToHex to clamp, round, and zero-pad a single color component.

symbols.does: Linearly interpolates between two hex colors.
symbols.description: Converts both colors to RGB, interpolates each channel by t, and returns the resulting hex string.

symbols.does: Checks whether a value looks like a 6-digit hex color.
symbols.description: Accepts an optional leading # and validates the remaining six hexadecimal characters.

symbols.does: Normalizes a hex string to #RRGGBB uppercase.
symbols.description: Trims the input, adds a leading # if missing, and converts the result to uppercase.

symbols.does: Converts a hex color to HSL components in the [0,1] range.
symbols.description: Computes hue, saturation, and lightness from the RGB representation using the standard max/min algorithm.

symbols.does: Converts HSL components to an uppercase hex string.
symbols.description: Uses the piecewise hue-to-RGB mapping and then converts the resulting RGB values to hex.

symbols.does: Local piecewise helper inside hslToHex.
symbols.description: Maps a normalized hue offset to an RGB channel value during HSL-to-RGB conversion.

symbols.does: Inverts only the lightness of a hex color.
symbols.description: Keeps hue and saturation unchanged and replaces lightness L with 1 - L, implementing the luminosity-slice polarity inversion.

symbols.does: Computes the four page surface colors from warmth and polarity dials.
symbols.description: Interpolates each SURF_KEY between cool and warm anchors by the warmth fraction, then optionally inverts lightness for dark polarity.

symbols.does: Writes the identity's CSS variables onto a document or element root.
symbols.description: Sets page, warm, cool, nested, accent, ink, semantic, and scaffold variables, applying invertL to luminance-role colors only when polarity is dark.

symbols.does: Local helper that conditionally inverts a hex for dark polarity.
symbols.description: Inside injectVars, returns invertL(hex) when polarity is dark and the value is a valid hex, otherwise returns the value unchanged.

symbols.does: Async GET helper for the local API.
symbols.description: Fetches JSON from a path with no-store caching and throws an error if the response is not OK.

symbols.does: Async POST helper for the local API.
symbols.description: Sends a JSON body, parses the response, and throws if the response is not OK or returns ok:false.

symbols.does: HTML-escapes a small set of characters.
symbols.description: Replaces &, <, >, and " with their corresponding HTML entities so user text can be safely inserted into HTML.

symbols.does: Maps an index in a collection to a color on the warm-metal ramp.
symbols.description: Uses piecewise hex interpolation across the axis ramp stops; falls back to FB.ramp if the tokens do not define one.

symbols.does: Mounts the Atlas navigation chip and panel on the current page.
symbols.description: Injects inline CSS, creates a fixed chip button, a scrim, and a slide-in panel, then wires open/close interactions.

symbols.does: Fetches atlas data and renders the Atlas panel content.
symbols.description: Loads surface/atlas.json, fetches live face versions, reads the manifest version, and builds the categorized list of surfaces, faces, documents, and proofs.

symbols.does: Shows the Atlas panel.
symbols.description: Lazily calls build on first open, then adds show classes to the scrim and panel.

symbols.does: Hides the Atlas panel.
symbols.description: Removes the show classes from the scrim and panel.

symbols.does: Renders a button from an application.components record.
symbols.description: Looks up the named component and state, then creates a button with geometry, fill, ink, border, and shadow drawn from the record.

symbols.does: Factory for a typed feedback record.
symbols.description: Returns an object with target, kind, and value, unifying preference, verdict, and note shapes.

symbols.does: Converts a legacy feedback entry into an array of marks.
symbols.description: Emits a preference, verdict, and note for the piece, plus a note mark for each comment that carries an element address and fractional position.

symbols.does: Lists dials declared to modify a given target.
symbols.description: Filters grammar.dials by targets.modifies, ignoring keys that start with an underscore.

symbols.does: Computes the combined spacing multiplier from relevant dials.
symbols.description: Multiplies the multipliers of every dial whose targets.modifies is spacing, using the current option value from opts.

symbols.does: Resolves a spacing bond to a pixel value.
symbols.description: Computes unit times the bond count times the density dial multiplier times the tier multiplier, rounding to an integer.

symbols.does: Writes the spacing scale as CSS variables onto a target root.
symbols.description: Sets both px-named count addresses and semantic bond addresses, resolving each through the density dial and tier.

symbols.does: Resolves a role's colour and type by walking registry pointers.
symbols.description: Uses dotted pointers against supplied tokens, grammar, roles, and application registries; returns resolved values and an unresolved array, with space flagged as pending tuning.

symbols.does: Local pointer-walker inside resolve.
symbols.description: Splits a dotted pointer and traverses the registries object, returning undefined if the path is invalid or the value is missing.

symbols.does: Derives a [workGrow, panelGrow] pair from a viewer layout config.
symbols.description: Handles full-work/full-panel endpoints, then reads the named grammar proportion archetype and orients the parts by the config's major side.

symbols.does: Creates a reusable resizable or fullscreenable two-slot split.
symbols.description: Manages fraction, full state, optional localStorage persistence, divider dragging, and applying flex styles to primary and secondary elements.

symbols.does: Local helper that persists the split fraction.
symbols.description: Inside splitPane, stores the current fraction to localStorage when opts.persist is set, or removes the key when the fraction is null.

symbols.does: Local helper that clamps a fraction to the configured range.
symbols.description: Inside splitPane, returns a value between clamp[0] and clamp[1], defaulting to 0.5 for null input.

symbols.does: Local helper that updates the split pane layout.
symbols.description: Inside splitPane, sets primary and secondary element styles based on the current fraction, full state, and whether the split is active.

symbols.does: Local pointer-move handler for divider dragging.
symbols.description: Inside splitPane, recalculates the fraction from the pointer position relative to the split rectangle, respecting orientation.

symbols.does: Local pointer-up handler for divider dragging.
symbols.description: Inside splitPane, ends the drag, removes window listeners, and persists the final fraction.

symbols.does: Attaches two-finger pinch-to-zoom handling to an element.
symbols.description: Tracks touch distance ratio, maps it onto a clamped zoom value via caller-supplied get/set, and notifies the caller on gesture start and end.

symbols.does: Local helper that computes touch distance.
symbols.description: Inside pinchZoom, returns the Euclidean distance between two touch points.

symbols.does: Local touch-end/cancel handler for pinch zoom.
symbols.description: Inside pinchZoom, signals the end of a pinch gesture when fewer than two touches remain.
